text: Savoury
Hearty
Succulent
A generous portion
Peckish
A craving
To cater for
To be in the mood for
To swap
Bland
To splurge on
To whip up
To box up
Good evening. Have you decided, or shall I run you through the specials?
I am really in the mood for something hearty tonight. What do you recommend?
Then the slow-roasted lamb. It is wonderfully succulent, with a generous portion of potatoes.
Perfect. Could I have it cooked without the garlic butter? And could I swap the potatoes for a salad?
Of course. We can easily cater for that. Anything to drink?
Just water, thank you. And honestly, I am only peckish, so I will skip the starter.
Understood. If you have a craving for something sweet later, the chef can whip up a small dessert.
That sounds lovely. If the portion is too big, could I have the rest boxed up?
Absolutely. It is such a popular dish that nobody ever leaves it behind. I will bring your water right away.

Good evening, and welcome. Before you choose, let me run you through tonight's specials. To start, we have a hearty pumpkin soup, beautifully savoury, with a generous portion of toasted seeds on top. For the main course, the chef is doing a succulent slow-roasted lamb that the whole table tends to fight over. Now, if you happen to have any dietary needs, do let me know. We can easily get the lamb prepared without the garlic butter, and we can have the risotto made completely dairy-free. The portions are quite large tonight, so if you are only feeling a little peckish, two starters often work better than a full main.

Honestly, dining out has become one of my favourite parts of travelling. When I was younger, I would just eat whatever was put in front of me. These days I am far more confident. If a dish comes with something I do not fancy, I simply ask to have it swapped. If the kitchen can cater for a craving, they usually will. The other night the portion was so generous that I could not finish it, and the food was such a treat that I asked the waiter to get the rest boxed up to take away. A good meal abroad is no longer something I am nervous about. It is something I look forward to.
